title: Building to Understand
summary: The most useful prototypes do not prove that an idea works. They reveal what the idea actually is.
body: Some things cannot be understood from the outside. You have to build a small version, push on its edges, and watch which assumptions collapse.
This is why the work collected here is allowed to remain unfinished. A prototype can succeed by producing a better question.

title: Interfaces for Long Projects
summary: A project that may last ten years needs different interfaces than one shipping next Friday.
body: Long projects need room for uncertainty, graceful archives, and a visible history of changed minds.

title: Notes on Creative Friction
summary: Constraints are only useful when they resist in the right direction.
body: The goal is not to remove every obstacle. It is to distinguish useful resistance from accidental drag.

title: Why Albums Still Matter
summary: An album is not a container for tracks. It is a designed period of attention.
body: Sequence creates meaning. The space between songs is part of the composition, and an ending changes everything that came before it.
